behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: request builder + API client wiring. A unit test of just string formatting can miss dropped fields at the client boundary. Real OS/network/LLM output is not required; a captured in-process fake client can catch the bug. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about request payload contents, not real HTTP effects or model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a fake API client that records outbound payload. Call classify with a taxonomy and assert every valid label is present in the sent request, exactly once, before dispatch.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure is local normalization/guard logic: mapping out-of-taxonomy values to "unknown". No real infra or model behavior needed. Rejected Integration: behavior text does not require cross-module state propagation; same failure is caught by testing the normalization boundary directly.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization/response-mapping logic with taxonomy [legal, technical, financial, other] and raw label like "medical"; assert output is "unknown" and no pass-through occurs.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic pure transformation behavior. Single function/object boundary, no I/O, no module interaction required.
behaviors.test_description: Run normalization twice (or property-style over repeated runs) with identical raw payload fixtures and assert identical final label each time.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on repeated live AI API outcomes for same input. A stubbed response always passes and cannot reveal model/provider variability. Not Workflow: repeated calls are parallel repetitions of one agentic decision, not a multi-step agent pipeline with dependent model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real calls against provider with fixed document + taxonomy, persist raw provider responses and final labels, then assert label changes only when raw provider output changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is coordination between tokenizer/budget policy/request dispatcher. Unit tests of truncation alone can miss dispatch still firing with over-budget payload. No real network/LLM output required.
behaviors.test_description: Use module wired with fake tokenizer (or deterministic tokenizer module) and fake API client. Feed over-budget document, assert configured policy outcome (truncate or reject) and verify outbound request respects budget or dispatch is blocked.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires a real provider call and real provider metering fields; mocks hide tokenizer/provider-side accounting mismatches. Per guidance, execution through real AI API defaults to Agentic unless purely wrapper infra, and this behavior is about provider-computed usage itself.
behaviors.test_description: Issue real classification call with representative document and taxonomy; assert provider-reported usage (prompt/total tokens per provider schema) is <= configured budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure is retry orchestration across classifier + API client + backoff/wait component. In-process fake client can emit rate-limit then success and expose missing retry logic. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real network timing/OS primitives to reproduce; protocol-level retry path can be validated locally.
behaviors.test_description: Wire module to fake client that returns rate-limit error first, success second, plus controllable clock/sleeper. Assert module waits per policy, retries, and returns successful label instead of surfacing the first error.
